assistant:
*Today's swim — Skills/Speed (50m pool, ~3,000m, ~58 min)*

*Warm-up (600m)*
• 400m easy free — settle in, long strokes

*Speed set (1,200m) — the main focus*
• 12×100m @ *1:32–1:34/100m*, 20s rest
• That's CSS−5 to −7 (CSS is 1:39). Faster than CSS but the job is to *hold it across all 12* — don't blast the first few and fade.
• Think clean, quick turnover, not muscling it. If you're slipping past 1:35 by rep 9, you went out too hard.

*Pull set (800m) — PB + paddles*
• 4×200m @ *1:36/100m* (CSS−3), 20s rest
• Buoy cuts leg drag, paddles load the catch — long, strong pull, no kick.
• This is repeatable at CSS−3, not a TT. Hold the pace, don't chase it down.
• Drop paddles for the last rep if your shoulders start loading up.

*Cool-down (200m)* easy mixed

*How to pace it:* speed set is about turnover and form under mild pressure; pull set is about strength and feel. Two different jobs — don't turn the pull reps into another speed effort.

Send me the splits after and I'll check the reps against target.

user: Is that decoupling or just the fact I'm warming up
assistant:
Largely warm-up — you've nailed it.

Decoupling splits the run in half and compares pace:HR efficiency first vs second. On a *28-min* run that maths is fragile: the first ~8–10 min is HR lag, your heart hasn't caught up to the pace yet, so HR sits artificially low. That makes the *first half look hyper-efficient*, which mechanically inflates the second-half "drift" — even if you were dead steady once warm.

So the 10.7% is mostly the warm-up ramp, not genuine cardiac drift. Two tells that back that up:
• Your *13.5 km* run yesterday — long enough for warm-up to wash out — decoupled *0.87%*. A truly drifting engine doesn't go from 0.87% to 10.7% overnight.
• avg HR 139, 90% in Z2, no late-run HR climb — the shape's flat once you're warm.

Rule of thumb: decoupling is only meaningful on runs *>60 min*. Under ~40 min, ignore it — the warm-up swamps the signal. Nothing to read into here.

• *Morning card didn't fire* — separate cron (the check-in), also missed this morning until you nudged.

I can't see the VM cron logs from here to tell you the root cause of the timeout (network round-trip to ICU, a slow Strava pull, or the job overlapping its own previous run).
